trust_boundary.reliance_statement: This adapter describes a simulated GRC workflow/risk/approval evidence shape. Workflow approvals are organizational decisions; they are not technical enforcement of a protected action.

control_surface.description: Simulated GRC platform emitting workflow/risk/approval decision records for declared change, access, or release requests. Approvals are governance signals, not runtime enforcement.

decision_event.description: Simulated GRC workflow/risk/approval decision evidence. This is a recorded organizational approval, not a runtime protected-action enforcement event.

bypass_evidence.limitation: GRC platforms record approvals; they do not observe whether the protected action was actually invoked through a controlled path. Bypass evidence must come from a gateway, SIEM, or native ProofRail bypass tester.

revocation_status.description: Workflow can record withdrawal of a prior approval; this is approval-level revocation, not runtime authority revocation.

subject_hashes.limitation: GRC workflow records typically reference subjects by identifier rather than by content hash; subject hashes should come from the manifest or a technical evidence source.

source_identity.description: Source identity is the GRC platform identity declared in adapter metadata; this is not a trust decision.

timestamp_integrity.limitation: GRC platforms record administrative timestamps; v0.2.6 does not verify clock trust or assert that workflow timestamps reflect runtime enforcement times.

normalization_notes: Maps GRC workflow outcomes (approved/rejected) to ProofRail allow/deny-style decision observations as workflow-level evidence only.
Maps workflow justification strings to the normalized reason field unchanged.
Does not assert that workflow approval implies the protected action was technically enforced at runtime.

adapter_limitations: Static descriptor only; no real GRC platform integration.
Workflow approval is not technical enforcement.
Workflow approval alone is not sufficient for protected-action reliance; technical decision evidence from a gateway, policy engine, or native ProofRail run is required.
Does not prove that the approved action was actually invoked through a controlled path.
non_claims: This adapter is not a certification.
This adapter does not make the GRC platform a trust authority.
This adapter does not assert that workflow approval substitutes for technical enforcement.
This adapter does not assert that any real GRC product is conformant.
